sanskrit: भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्यमहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन । ज्ञातुं दृष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परंतप ॥
hindiTranslation: परन्तु हे शत्रुतापन अर्जुन  इस प्रकार चतुर्भुजरूपवाला मैं अनन्यभक्तिसे ही तत्त्वसे जाननेमें, सगुणरूपसे देखनेमें और प्राप्त करनेमें शक्य हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: But only through undivided devotion can I be truly known, seen, and entered, Arjuna.
essence: Undivided devotion reaches what effort cannot.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says that his true form can be known, seen, and entered only through single-pointed devotion, not by ordinary effort alone.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing the deepest reality responds to love and single-heartedness, not just achievement.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna has just seen Krishna's cosmic form and is overwhelmed. Krishna now explains the final key: this presence cannot be grasped by learning or ritual alone. Only undivided devotion can truly know and enter him.
modernRelevance: You can read every book, gather every technique, and still miss what matters. The moment you stop trying to control everything and give yourself fully, understanding begins.
translationLiteral: भक्त्या (through devotion) / तु (but) / अनन्यया (undivided) / शक्यः (possible) / अहम् (I) / एवंविधः (of this kind) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / ज्ञातुम् (to know) / द्रष्टुम् (to see) / च (and) / तत्त्वेन (in essence) / प्रवेष्टुम् (to enter) / च (and) / परंतप (O scorcher of foes)
insight: It shifts the mind from mastery by force to access through wholehearted devotion.

quoteVariants.text: Only undivided devotion can know, see, and enter me.
Devotion, not effort alone, reaches the deepest reality.
